rag_rules: AGREE contracts for component ports must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes
Implementation-level bindings like assignments should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and should be placed in appropriate AADL component type or implementation scopes
Guarantees in component type annexes must reference only visible ports and features, not implementation-only subcomponent paths
Generated AGREE code must use only identifiers and types present in the analyzed AADL model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` syntax within annex blocks
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence patterns like `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; use implementation annexes instead
Do not reference package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the input AADL model
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
AGREE logical operators and expression patterns
